name: Lu Su
description: Lu Su (172–217), courtesy name Zijing, was a military general and strategist serving under the warlord Sun Quan during the late Eastern Han dynasty of China. Renowned for his strategic vision and integrity, Lu Su played a crucial role in the establishment and consolidation of the state of Eastern Wu during the Three Kingdoms period. He initially served under Yuan Shu but later defected to Sun Quan after recognizing his superior leadership. Lu Su succeeded Zhou Yu as the Grand Commander of Eastern Wu's forces following Zhou Yu's death, continuing his predecessor's policy of alliance with Liu Bei against Cao Cao. He strongly advocated for maintaining this alliance, understanding that a divided China was the only way for Eastern Wu to survive. His wisdom and diplomatic skills were instrumental in navigating the complex political landscape of the time, preventing Eastern Wu from being absorbed by the larger, more powerful kingdoms of Wei and Shu. He is remembered as a selfless and insightful strategist, dedicated to the well-being of his lord and his people, and a key figure in shaping the course of the Three Kingdoms era. Unlike some of his contemporaries, Lu Su was known for his generosity and willingness to help those in need, further solidifying his reputation as a virtuous and respected leader. He is often contrasted with the more aggressive and ambitious figures of the era, highlighting his commitment to peace and stability.
subtitle: Wise Strategist of Eastern Wu
achievements: Advising Sun Quan on key strategic decisions

enemies.name: Those who threatened the Sun-Liu alliance

keyMyths.title: Lu Su Succeeds Zhou Yu
keyMyths.description: Following Zhou Yu's death, Lu Su was chosen by Sun Quan to succeed him as Grand Commander. This demonstrated Sun Quan's trust in Lu Su's abilities and commitment to the alliance.

keyMyths.title: Maintaining the Sun-Liu Alliance
keyMyths.description: Lu Su understood the importance of the alliance between Sun Quan and Liu Bei to counter Cao Cao's dominance. He worked tirelessly to preserve the alliance, even in the face of disagreements and betrayals.

keyMyths.title: Lu Su's Generosity
keyMyths.description: Lu Su was known for his generosity and willingness to help those in need. This made him a popular figure among the people and strengthened his position within Eastern Wu.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: The primary historical source for the Three Kingdoms period.
sources.text: The primary historical source for the Three Kingdoms period.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A fictionalized account of the Three Kingdoms period, popularizing many of the key figures and events.
sources.text: A fictionalized account of the Three Kingdoms period, popularizing many of the key figures and events.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: Lu Su's unwavering allegiance to Sun Quan and Eastern Wu exemplifies the Confucian ideal of loyalty to one's lord.

associations.name: Strategic Acumen
associations.significance: His ability to foresee long-term strategic advantages, such as the alliance with Liu Bei, highlights his exceptional military intelligence.

associations.name: Integrity
associations.significance: Lu Su was known for his honesty and fairness, earning him the respect of allies and adversaries alike.

associations.name: Bridge Builder
associations.significance: Symbolic of Lu Su's role in forging and maintaining the alliance between Sun Quan and Liu Bei.

associations.name: Diplomacy
associations.significance: Lu Su's diplomatic skills were crucial in navigating the complex political landscape of the Three Kingdoms period.

associations.name: Water
associations.significance: Represents the fluidity and adaptability of his strategies, as well as the naval power of Eastern Wu.

associations.name: Crane
associations.significance: The crane symbolizes longevity, wisdom, and good fortune, reflecting Lu Su's lasting impact and strategic foresight.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both Odysseus and Lu Su were known for their strategic thinking and diplomatic skills in navigating complex political landscapes.
Similar to Agrippa's role in securing Augustus's power, Lu Su was instrumental in establishing and consolidating Sun Quan's rule.
Like Bragi, the Norse god of poetry and wisdom, Lu Su possessed great intellect and foresight, guiding his lord with sage advice.
Both were long-lived advisors to emperors, known for their wisdom and loyalty.

worshipPractices.name: Ancestral Shrines
worshipPractices.description: In some regions, families with connections to Lu Su may maintain ancestral shrines to honor his memory and seek his blessings for strategic guidance and success.

worshipPractices.name: Local Temples
worshipPractices.description: Certain local temples dedicated to historical figures of the Three Kingdoms period may include Lu Su among those venerated for their contributions to the region.

cultural.modernLegacy: Lu Su remains a respected figure in Chinese culture, often depicted in operas, literature, and video games as a wise and loyal strategist. His emphasis on alliances and long-term planning continues to be studied in military strategy and leadership courses.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Service Under Yuan Shu
extendedContent.content: Lu Su was born in Dongcheng County. From a young age, he displayed remarkable intelligence and a keen interest in military strategy. He was known for his generosity and willingness to help those in need, earning him the respect of his local community. Initially, Lu Su served under the warlord Yuan Shu, but he quickly recognized Yuan Shu's lack of vision and ambition. Disappointed with Yuan Shu's leadership, Lu Su sought a more promising leader who could bring stability and prosperity to the region. His reputation for intelligence and integrity preceded him, making him a sought-after advisor among the various warlords vying for power during the tumultuous late Eastern Han dynasty. Lu Su carefully observed the political landscape, seeking a leader with both the vision and the ability to unite the fragmented territories and restore order to the land. His decision to leave Yuan Shu marked a turning point in his career, setting him on a path that would ultimately lead him to serve under Sun Quan and play a pivotal role in the establishment of Eastern Wu.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Strategic Contributions
extendedContent.content: Lu Su's greatest contribution was his strategic vision in advocating for and maintaining the alliance between Sun Quan and Liu Bei against Cao Cao. Recognizing the threat posed by Cao Cao's overwhelming military strength, Lu Su convinced Sun Quan of the necessity of forming an alliance with Liu Bei, despite the inherent risks and potential for future conflicts. This alliance proved crucial in the Battle of Red Cliffs, where the combined forces of Sun Quan and Liu Bei decisively defeated Cao Cao's numerically superior army, preventing him from conquering the south and laying the foundation for the Three Kingdoms period. Lu Su also played a vital role in securing Jing Province for Sun Quan after the death of Zhou Yu. His diplomatic skills and strategic maneuvering allowed Sun Quan to gain control of this strategically important territory without resorting to open warfare with Liu Bei, further solidifying Eastern Wu's position in the south. These quests cemented his legacy as a brilliant strategist and diplomat.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Impact on the Three Kingdoms
extendedContent.content: Lu Su's legacy extends beyond his military achievements. He was known for his integrity, foresight, and ability to see the bigger picture. His emphasis on alliances and long-term strategic planning had a profound impact on the course of the Three Kingdoms period. Even after his death, his strategic vision continued to guide Eastern Wu's policies. Lu Su's contributions were instrumental in shaping the political landscape of the Three Kingdoms, and his legacy continues to be celebrated in Chinese culture. He embodied the Confucian ideals of loyalty, righteousness, and strategic wisdom, making him a revered figure in Chinese history. His ability to navigate the complex political landscape of the Three Kingdoms and forge alliances that benefited his lord and his people cemented his place as one of the most influential strategists of his time. His story serves as a reminder of the importance of strategic thinking, diplomacy, and integrity in achieving long-term success.

shortDescription: A brilliant strategist and diplomat, Lu Su secured the alliance between Sun Quan and Liu Bei, crucial for the survival of Eastern Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
